executive_summary.main_recommendation: AI spend appears productive overall but unevenly allocated: total spend is 81.7% of budget, forecast to decline 5.6% next month, while productivity gain is 17.5%. The highest-value action is to reduce or tightly control overspender usage where token consumption materially outruns peer productivity, and to replicate the workflows of high_roi and efficient users whose positioning index is strongly positive. A secondary action is adoption coaching for low_adoption users, because their low token usage suggests unrealized value is more likely than meaningful budget waste. Given bugs reduction is -59.6%, keep quality-risk users under review and avoid expanding their AI budget until delivery quality and output conversion improve.

employee_metrics.name: Leila Park
employee_metrics.recommendation: Leila Park is a clear benchmark: rank #1/40 in story points (P100), rank #2/40 in productivity (P98), and only P12 in token usage, giving a positioning index of +85. This indicates highly productive spend rather than excess cost, so replicate this workflow across the team by documenting prompt structure, context selection, and task sequencing. What should be replicated is the ability to convert relatively low token usage into top-tier delivery and productivity. Next month KPI to watch: story_points.

employee_metrics.name: Amina Blanc
employee_metrics.recommendation: Amina Blanc ranks #14/40 in story points (P68) and #5/40 in productivity (P90) while token usage is only P20, for a positioning index of +70. This is productive spend, and what should be replicated is her prompt discipline, context-selection habits, and task patterns that generate above-peer output with below-peer usage. Action: replicate workflow across similar mid-volume users rather than constrain budget. Next month KPI to watch: productivity_percentile.
